item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: poor dicks. :-( I'll pour one out for him. enthusiastic +1. I would LOVE to read more about this character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love Totleigh Soft. Never stop. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are not bug, are features

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for weird and ridiculous Garry's Mod man. Reminded me of when I was younger when I'd watch GMod videos all the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The visual is perfect. It's like an anomalous Garry's Mod. Everything I could ever hope and dream of such a thing.

I very nearly choked on my lunch earlier, reading this on my phone.

Frankly, I for one would appreciate a new TotleighSoft production making it's way into containment around every E3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I giggled,I smirked, I sighed and cringed in sympathy, I voted UP. Poor dicks McSquigee!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, this is quite… Accurate. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Did… did you fucking pirate me?

Goddammit, I laughed so hard.

I'm generally positive toward TotleighSoft articles, but even I have to agree, this is a good one. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is essentially every an0nymoose and RubberFruit video turned into one person, who gets thrown into the real world.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My broader point being, the hints are there, but they're not worked in or successfully indicative of a broader story, and they don't make sense with what's happened. They just kind of roll out randomly, which would be less of a problem if they weren't a significant focus of the SCP.

Edit: Like, it literally ends, "I'M A BOOTLEG", curtains close, hold for applause. But the journey to "I'm a bootleg" was kind of forced and abrupt and uneven, so that's not a satisfying ending. We know somebody bootlegged him, I guess, but there's nothing to imply who is responsible poor any kind of past narrative, so effectively that's the whole story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems more like GAW, or a deniable op by MTF Kappa-10 ("Skynet").

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could be GAW? Could be an independent party? Could be anyone, frankly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWCY? Is it you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well the Foundation just took it, Totleighsoft didn't sell it to them.

There's also the irregularities with the patch notes, and the fact that he speaks English, and the name change, which all seem to hint that dicks was pirated and modified, but not by the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
poor, poor dicks mcSquigee

+1 out of sympathy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole "I'm an illegal bootleg" plotline doesn't make any sense to me, because they know exactly how and where they got him. They physically detained him as a demo at an E3 booth.

If you're trying to make some kind of metaphorical comparison between collecting him physically and acquiring software without a licence, that didn't come across. If you're trying to imply that the E3 demo was bootlegged or illegitimate, that also didn't come across.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
dicks mcSquigee

Reminds me of all the dumb names one gives to the Pokemon game rivals.

TotleighSoft

I don't call myself welcoming of that GoI, but this article proves that just maybe, it might have at least the slightest bit of broader applicability.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Can We call him John or whatever? He's been though enough…

(oh +1 btw)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked Mr. dicks mcSquigee when you first showed me this draft, and I like him even more now.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's my name, don't wear it out.

item.title: is mine.
item.content:encoded:
NEW psysics by TotleighSoft!
NEW design with reality, TotleighSoft PSYSICSTM let you GREATER game!
Try MORE dimemensions for MUCHER experience.
Confines are NONE but MIND!

- Note cut from the final version.

Inspired by the idea of someone 'struggling to exist in three-dimensional space.'

Thanks to everyone for reviewing and reading, I hope you all enjoy it.
